text.title: AI Feature GA Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every application of AI Feature GA Checklist enforces. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every cycle, run every item in the AI Feature GA Checklist list — no silent skips. A skipped item requires a written one-line reason logged with the cycle artefact.

rationale.source: ops checklist research / Atul Gawande, The Checklist Manifesto (2009)

rule.rationale: Hygiene activities only compound when consistent. Survivorship bias makes 'skipped this week' invisible until the third quarter when the founder finds three months of unchecked AR.

rule.statement: Anchor each item to a specific named source-of-truth (system, dashboard, doc URL). Items without a named source are speculation, not checks.
rule.rationale: Items like 'review marketing health' are unactionable. 'Check ad-account CPA in Google Ads → Reports → last-7-days vs prior-7-days' is.

rule.statement: Cap the entire pass at the time-box (e.g., 30 min weekly, half-day annual). Items that don't fit get split or deferred — never crammed.
rule.rationale: Over-budget checklists are abandoned by week 4. Strict caps force prioritization and surface real-vs-busywork items.

rule.statement: Carry forward open items to the next cycle with an explicit `aging` counter. Items older than 3 cycles trigger escalation or removal.

rationale.source: retrospective best practices / Esther Derby, Agile Retrospectives (2006)

rule.rationale: Stale checklist items hide structural problems (missing capability, wrong owner). Aging surfaces them.

example.title: Anchored checklist item
example.code: Item: 'Cash position check' Source: Bank dashboard → Operating account, today's balance Action: Compare to prior week and 12-week trend Decision: If runway <6 months, flag to founder this week
example.note: Each field — source, action, decision — is unambiguous. The rater knows exactly what to do.

example.title: Vague checklist item
example.code: Item: 'Check financial health' Action: Look at the numbers
example.note: Two raters will inspect different systems on different cadences. The item appears done while nothing actionable was produced.
